runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_question: How do you use basic r syntax for calculations?
runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_answer: You use basic R syntax for calculations by entering expressions in the console and assign variables using the assignment operator.
accepted_variations: How do you use basic r syntax for calculations?

runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_question: How do you name vectors in R?
runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_answer: You can name vectors in R by assigning names to their elements using the names() function.
accepted_variations: How do you name vectors in R?

runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_question: How do you compare vectors in R?
runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_answer: You can compare vectors in R using comparison operators to evaluate relationships between their elements.
accepted_variations: How do you compare vectors in R?

runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_question: How do you name lists in R?
runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_answer: You can name lists in R by assigning names to their elements.
accepted_variations: How do you name lists in R?

runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_question: How do you apply r skills to analyze simple real-world datasets?
runtime_precomputed_qa_pairs.canonical_answer: You apply R skills to analyze simple real-world datasets by practicing with real data sets, such as analyzing gambling results using vectors or examining box office numbers of movies.
accepted_variations: How do you apply r skills to analyze simple real-world datasets?
